CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JAN-07-2026 CONTINUED TO MAR-11-2026 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 301, HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING, AS DISCUSSED AND MODIFIED BY AGREEMENT RE PLAINTIFF'S DEADLINE TO FILE AND SERVE THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF XIAO YU IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CLAIM AGAINST BART. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE AND SERVE THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. A PROPOSED ORDER IS SUBMITTED. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK) ***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS EXECUTED.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances:
Jerome Bellotti, Esq., of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc., via Zoom videoconference, 408.512.3600, for Plaintiff.

Peter Van Zandt, Esq, of Allen, Glaessner, Hazelwood & Werth, LLP, in person, 415.697-2000, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling, as discussed and modified by agreement re Plaintiff's deadline to file and serve the second amended complaint, as follows: Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District's demurrer to the sole cause of action for negligence alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Xiao Yu is sustained with leave to amend for the final opportunity to allege a viable claim against BART. Plaintiff fails to allege any ultimate facts supporting an exception to the general rule that public entities do not owe any duty to protect individuals from criminal conduct committed by third parties. Plaintiff must file and serve the second amended complaint within 20 days of service of the Notice of Entry of this Order.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To May-13-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2025 Continued To Mar-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The unopposed demurrer to the first and second causes of action (negligence and dangerous condition of public property, respectively) is sustained with 20 days' leave to amend. In the event a subsequent pleadings motion is filed, Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with the meet and confer obligation. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.41.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The unopposed motion to strike is denied without prejudice as moot in light of the court's companion order today sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend. In the event a subsequent pleadings motion is filed, Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with the meet and confer obligation. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 435.5.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
